zh.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham於1345年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可內特萊斯亞德。她於1362年之前與Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser在英國林可爾史爾格爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她埋葬在英國素福爾可登維其格也福利亞斯 普利奧利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham于1345年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可內特萊斯亞德。她于1362年之前与Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser在英国林可爾史爾格爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她埋葬在英国素福爾可登維其格也福利亞斯 普利奧利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cobham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of at least three places in southern England called Cobham, in Kent, Surrey, and Sussex.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: (Elizabeth)
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham was born in 1345, in Nettlestead, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. She married Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser before 1362, in Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She was buried in Greyfriars Priory, Dunwich, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham est née en 1345, à Nettlestead, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser avant 1362, à Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elleenterrée à Greyfriars Priory, Dunwich, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham wurde 1345 in Nettlestead, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat vor 1362 in Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie wurde in Greyfriars Priory, Dunwich, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham è nata il nel 1345 nel Nettlestead, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser prima del 1362, nel Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Fu sepolta a Greyfriars Priory, Dunwich, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobhamは1345年にイギリスイギリススフォルクネットレステアドで生まれた。1362年以前にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアゴクスヒルでPhilip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenserと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリススフォルクダンウィチグレイフリアルズ プリオリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham(은)는 1345년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 네틀레스테아드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1362년 이전 영국 링코실르 고스힐에서 Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 수폴크 던위치 그리프리아르스 플리오리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham nasceu em 1345, em Nettlestead, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela casou-se com Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser antes 1362, em Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela foi sepultada em Greyfriars Priory, Dunwich, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham родилась в 1345 г. в местности неттлестид, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser перед 1362 г. в местности гоксхилл, линколншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она была похоронена в местности грифрярс приори, дунвич, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham nació en 1345, en Nettlestead, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser antes de 1362, en Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Fue sepultada en Greyfriars Priory, Dunwich, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
